findings.title: Creator-coin catalog overloads poolId and drops the V4 PoolId
findings.description: The new public catalog entry exposes `poolId` as the internal fixture/config id, while the source `UniswapV4PoolConfig` also has a canonical bytes32 V4 `poolId` that is not emitted. The checked-in JSON and Solidity test now enshrine `poolId: "uniswap-v4-basedflick-zora"`, so a downstream consumer cannot mechanically compare the catalog against the onchain PoolId and may reasonably misread this field as the V4 PoolId. This is exactly the sort of schema ambiguity that is painful to fix after consumers start depending on the artifact.
findings.suggested_fix: Rename the catalog's config identifier to `poolConfigId` and add a separate `v4PoolId: Hex` populated from the V4 pool config, then update the generated JSON and Foundry artifact assertions to verify both fields.

residual_risks: Route and gap-matrix `capabilities` gain required fields under schemaVersion 1. This appears intentionally additive, but strict external JSON consumers with exact schemas could still reject the artifacts.
The creator-coin catalog currently starts from committed config and evidence labels only; it does not yet implement live fleet-style import/discovery.
testing_gaps: Foundry verifies the catalog hash, entry count, selected policy fields, and route ABI parity, but it does not yet assert the catalog's canonical V4 PoolId because that field is absent.
Verified locally: `bun run verify` in `router-ts` passed, and `forge test --match-path test/router/FameRouterGeneratedArtifacts.t.sol` passed.
